name: Cajun-Style Crab Cakes W/Creole Honey-Mustard Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 teaspoon Cajun spice mix, or see directions
4 slices smoked bacon, cooked and crumbled
recipeInstructions:
Crabcakes:
1. Tear up the bread slices and grind in a blender or food processor.
2. Add eggs, heavy cream, and lemon juice to form a heavy batter.
3. Remove to a bowl and add all remaining ingredients except dried breadcrumbs, oil, and sauce.
4. Form into small cakes about 1-2 inches in diameter.
5. Carefully coat them with the dried breadcrumbs.
6. Heat a large skillet or sauté pan with 1/2 inch of vegetable oil over medium heat.
7. Fry some of the cakes until golden brown on both sides, then place in a 200°F oven to keep warm.
8. Repeat until all cakes are fried.
Makes 24 cakes, serves 12 as an appetizer.
Creole Honey-Mustard Sauce:
1. In a small non-reactive saucepan over medium heat, heat oil and sauté peppercorns, black pepper, and shallots for about 3 minutes or until shallots are translucent.
2. Add white wine and simmer until the wine has almost completely evaporated.
3. Let cool. Add remaining ingredients and check seasoning.
Makes about 1 1/2 cups.
Chef's Tip:
Cajun spice mix (usually containing oregano, paprika, cayenne pepper, onion powder, and salt) can be purchased at the supermarket or mixed at home to suit your preference.
Chef's Tip:
This dipping sauce will keep up to one week when stored covered in the refrigerator.
